Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act, to authorize certain payments to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and to amend another Act
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act, to authorize certain payments to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and to amend another Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act, to authorize certain payments to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund and to amend ...
Identification.BillSponsor: DEPUTY PRIME MINISTER AND MINISTER OF FINANCE

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act to authorize additional payments to the provinces and territories. It also authorizes payments to be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund in relation to Canadaâs COVID-19 immunization plan and infrastructure and amends the heading of Part 9 of the Keeping Canadaâs Economy and Jobs Growing Act.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by adding the following after section 24.71:
The Minister may pay an additional cash payment equal to

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance may make the following payments to the provinces and territories in respect of Canadaâs COVID-19 immunization plan:
Any amount payable under subsection (1) may be paid by the Minister of Finance out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at the times and in the manner that the Minister of Finance considers appropriate.

Section.Text: Despite section 161 of the Keeping Canadaâs Economy and Jobs Growing Act, as amended by section 233 of the Economic Action Plan 2013 Act, No. 1, there may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Infrastructure and Communities or the Minister of Indigenous Services, in accordance with terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, in addition to the sum referred to in that section 161, a sum not exceeding $2,200,000,000 to provinces, territories, municipalities, municipal associations, provincial, territorial and municipal entities and First Nations for the purpose of municipal, regional and First Nations infrastructure.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Keeping Canadaâs Economy and Jobs Growing Act

Section.Text: The heading of Part 9 of the Keeping Canadaâs Economy and Jobs Growing Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Canada Community-Building Fund â Financing Municipal Infrastructure

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Keeping Canadaâs Economy and Jobs Growing Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 4:

Heading.TitleText: Gas Tax Fund â Financing Municipal Infrastructure
